doc_description: The "ShadowLearn User Manual" provides comprehensive guidance on utilizing the ShadowLearn application for language learning through the shadowing technique, detailing setup instructions, API key management, lesson creation, and interactive features with an AI companion for personalized learning experiences.

structure.title: ShadowLearn User Manual
structure.summary: The partial document is a user manual for the ShadowLearn application, which utilizes the shadowing technique to aid users in learning foreign languages. It provides an overview of the app's features, emphasizing the combination of listening, repeating, and interactive exercises with AI assistance. The manual includes instructions for initial setup, specifically for beginners, detailing the requirement for API keys and a PIN during the first launch. It explains the purpose of the API keys, including the OpenRouter API Key for translation and grammar assistance, and the Google Gemini API Key for enabling live voice conversations. The document also assures users of the security of their API keys, stating that they are encrypted and stored locally on the user's computer.

nodes.title: Welcome to ShadowLearn!
nodes.summary: The partial document is a user manual for the ShadowLearn application, which utilizes the shadowing technique (Listen & Repeat) to aid users in learning foreign languages. It provides an overview of the app's features, emphasizing the integration of smart interactive exercises and AI assistance. The manual includes instructions for initial setup, specifically for beginners, detailing the requirement for API keys and a PIN during the first launch. It explains the purpose of the API keys, including the OpenRouter API Key for translation and grammar assistance, and the Google Gemini API Key for enabling live voice conversations. The document also assures users of the security of their API keys, stating that they are encrypted and stored locally on the user's computer without being sent to the app's servers.

nodes.title: Initial Setup (For Beginners)
nodes.summary: The partial document is a user manual for the ShadowLearn application, which utilizes the shadowing technique (Listen & Repeat) to aid users in learning foreign languages. It provides an overview of the app's features and guides users through the initial setup process, specifically for beginners. The setup involves entering API Keys and a PIN, which are necessary for connecting the app to AI models that facilitate translation, speech recognition, and pronunciation. The document emphasizes the importance of these API Keys, detailing their roles and ensuring users that all keys are encrypted and stored securely on their devices.

nodes.title: Key Name Main Role Importance

nodes.title: How to Get API Keys
nodes.summary: The partial document provides a guide on obtaining API keys for the OpenRouter and Google Gemini services. It includes step-by-step instructions for creating a Google Gemini API key, emphasizing the importance of using a paid billing method for stable performance. Additionally, it outlines the process of setting up a PIN for encrypting keys and lesson data, highlighting the necessity of remembering the PIN as there is no recovery option. The document also mentions the daily use of the app, where users must enter their PIN to unlock access to lessons and data, ensuring data protection when the app is not in use.

nodes.title: Create a PIN
nodes.summary: The partial document provides a guide on obtaining API keys for the OpenRouter and Google Gemini services. It includes step-by-step instructions for creating a Google Gemini API key, emphasizing the importance of using a paid billing method for stable performance. Additionally, it outlines the process of setting up a PIN for encrypting keys and lesson data, highlighting the necessity of remembering the PIN as there is no recovery option. The document also mentions the daily use of the app, where users must enter their PIN to access lessons and data, ensuring data protection when the app is closed.

structure.title: Daily Use: Unlocking the App
structure.summary: The partial document provides a guide on obtaining API keys for the OpenRouter and Google Gemini services, including step-by-step instructions for creating and managing these keys. It emphasizes the importance of setting up billing for reliable performance and warns against using free trials due to instability. Additionally, it covers the process of creating a secure PIN to protect user data and API keys within the ShadowLearn app. The document also outlines features related to lesson management, including creating new lessons via YouTube links or file uploads, managing existing lessons, and utilizing the learning interface with playback controls.

structure.title: Library & Lesson Management
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the features of the Library and Lesson Management system within an educational app. It describes how to create a new lesson using either a YouTube link or by uploading audio/video files from a computer, including the automatic generation of subtitles. The document also details the management of existing lessons, allowing users to rename, delete, or retry processing failed lessons, as well as search for lessons by title. Additionally, it introduces the Learning Interface, which divides the screen into three columns, with a focus on the left column that features a video/audio player. This player includes controls for playback, speed adjustment, volume, and options to download or rename the lesson.

nodes.title: Creating a New Lesson
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines the features of the Library and Lesson Management system within an educational app. It describes how to create a new lesson either by pasting a YouTube link or uploading an audio/video file from a computer, with automatic subtitle generation for YouTube content. The document also details the management options for existing lessons, including renaming, deleting, retrying failed processes, and searching for lessons. Additionally, it introduces the Learning Interface, which features a video/audio player with controls for playback, speed adjustment, volume, and file downloading.

nodes.title: Middle Column — Subtitles & Practice
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines features of a language learning platform called ShadowLearn. It describes the middle column, which includes subtitles with Pinyin, Chinese text, and translations, allowing users to interact with video segments by clicking, hovering for pronunciation, bookmarking words, and starting shadowing sessions. The right column features an AI Companion, a personalized tutor that tracks vocabulary, progress, and runs interactive exercises. It highlights the differences between a standard chatbot and the AI Companion, emphasizing its ability to remember user preferences, adapt to learning levels, and conduct exercises directly in the chat. The document also provides suggestions for initiating conversations with the AI tutor.

It describes the middle column, which includes subtitles with Pinyin, Chinese text, and translations, allowing users to interact with video segments by clicking, hovering for pronunciation, bookmarking words, and starting shadowing sessions. nodes.title: How to Start a Conversation
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines the features and functionalities of the ShadowLearn language learning platform, focusing on its interactive components. It describes the middle column, which includes subtitles, Pinyin, Chinese text, and translations, allowing users to navigate video segments and engage with vocabulary through bookmarking and shadowing sessions. The right column features an AI Companion that acts as a personal language tutor, capable of tracking user progress, adapting exercises, and providing real-time feedback. The document highlights the AI's ability to remember user preferences and learning goals, facilitating personalized learning experiences. It also details various interactive exercises available in the chat, such as dictation, character writing, and pronunciation practice. Additionally, the AI can maintain a long-term memory of user information and progress, enabling tailored support in future sessions. Lastly, it introduces a voice conversation feature for real-time practice, offering grammar feedback and suggestions during interactions.

structure.title: Speak with AI — Live Voice Practice
structure.summary:
The partial document outlines features of an AI language learning tool focused on enhancing long-term memory and real-time voice practice. Key points include:

1. **Long-Term Memory**: The AI retains important user information such as learning goals, difficult topics, teaching preferences, and performance observations, which can be accessed in future sessions.

2. **Progress Tracking**: Users can request visual progress charts and skill breakdowns at any time to monitor their learning journey.

3. **Tips for Effective Use**: Specificity in requests, communicating mood, asking for alternative explanations, and trusting the AI's suggestions are recommended for optimal learning.

4. **Live Voice Practice**: The AI offers a real-time voice conversation feature that provides grammar corrections and next-line suggestions, allowing users to practice speaking without judgment or scheduling constraints. 

Overall, the document emphasizes personalized learning and immediate feedback to enhance language acquisition.

nodes.title: What You Get in Every Session
nodes.summary:
The partial document outlines features of an AI language learning tool focused on enhancing long-term memory, tracking progress, and facilitating real-time voice practice. Key points include:

1. **Long-Term Memory**: The AI retains information about the user's learning goals, difficulties, teaching preferences, and performance observations, which can be accessed in future sessions.

2. **Progress Tracking**: Users can request visual progress charts and skill breakdowns at any time to monitor their development in various language skills.

3. **Effective Interaction Tips**: Suggestions for maximizing the learning experience include being specific in requests, communicating mood, asking for alternative explanations, and trusting the AI's spaced repetition suggestions.

4. **Live Voice Practice**: The "Speak with AI" feature allows users to engage in real-time conversations with the AI, which provides grammar corrections and contextual support.

5. **Session Structure**: Each session includes voice conversation, grammar feedback, next-line suggestions, cultural tips, and a session recap summarizing performance and corrections.

6. **User Guidance**: Detailed steps for accessing and utilizing the voice practice feature, including language selection, persona choice, scenario selection, and session review.

7. **Shadowing Practice**: The document also describes shadowing modes for pronunciation and listening practice, detailing the dictation and speaking modes, along with the process for each.

Overall, the document serves as a user manual for effectively utilizing the AI language learning tool's features to enhance language acquisition.

structure.title: Shadowing Practice (Pronunciation & Listening)
structure.summary: The partial document provides a recap of a session focused on troubleshooting common issues and shadowing practice for pronunciation and listening skills. Key troubleshooting points include resolving a "Google API key required" error, fixing microphone issues in Chrome/Edge, addressing session loading problems by checking internet connectivity, and improving audio quality by closing other audio tabs or switching to a wired connection. The document also outlines the shadowing practice feature, detailing two modes: Dictation Mode, where users listen and type what they hear, and Speaking Mode, where users listen and speak aloud. It describes the flow of the Dictation Mode, including listening, typing, and revealing results, along with feedback on accuracy.

nodes.title: Choosing a Mode
nodes.summary: The partial document covers troubleshooting tips for common issues encountered during a session, such as adding a Google API key, microphone settings, and internet connection stability. It details the shadowing practice feature, explaining two modes: Dictation Mode and Speaking Mode, along with their respective phases for listening, typing, and speaking. The document also outlines how to access and utilize a Vocabulary Workbook, including saving words, searching, and practicing through quizzes. Additionally, it describes the quiz formats available for vocabulary practice.

structure.title: Vocabulary Workbook, Quizzes & Progress
structure.summary:
The partial document outlines the "Speaking Mode Flow" for a language learning application, detailing a three-step process: 

1. **Listen Phase**: Users listen to audio segments and can replay them as needed.
2. **Speak Phase**: Users record their pronunciation by clicking a microphone button, with options to playback or redo their recordings before submission.
3. **Reveal Phase**: Users receive feedback on their pronunciation, with a grading system indicating performance.

Additionally, it includes a summary of the shadowing session, showing accuracy scores and segment attempts. The document also describes a Vocabulary Workbook feature, where users can save words, access a full workbook, and practice through quizzes with various formats to enhance their vocabulary skills.

nodes.title: Saving Words
nodes.summary:
The partial document outlines the "Speaking Mode Flow" for a language learning application, detailing a three-step process: 

1. **Listen Phase**: Users listen to audio segments and can replay them as needed.
2. **Speak Phase**: Users record their spoken responses, with options to playback, redo, or submit their recordings.
3. **Reveal Phase**: Users receive feedback on their pronunciation, with a grading system indicating performance.

Additionally, it covers the "Shadowing Session Summary," which provides users with an accuracy score for each segment and an overview of their performance. The document also describes features related to a Vocabulary Workbook, including saving words, accessing the full workbook, and various quiz formats for practicing vocabulary. Users can bookmark words, search for them, hear pronunciations, and choose different exercise types for quizzes.

nodes.title: Accessing the Full Workbook
nodes.summary: The partial document outlines the features and functionalities of a language learning application, specifically focusing on the "Speaking Mode Flow" and the "Vocabulary Workbook." It details a three-step process for practicing speaking skills: listening to audio segments, recording spoken responses, and receiving feedback on pronunciation. Additionally, it describes how users can save vocabulary words, access a comprehensive workbook, and engage in various quiz formats to enhance their language skills. The document also highlights progress tracking features, including accuracy scores, skill breakdowns, and common mistakes, as well as a spaced repetition system for vocabulary review. Lastly, it provides information on settings that users can adjust, such as API keys and default translation languages.

structure.title: Settings
structure.summary: The partial document outlines the features and functionalities of the ShadowLearn platform, focusing on exercise skills tracked, scoring methods, common mistakes, spaced repetition, review queue, settings, and frequently asked questions (FAQ). It details various exercise types such as character writing, translation, and dictation, along with their scoring criteria. The document explains how the platform uses spaced repetition to optimize vocabulary review based on user performance. Additionally, it provides instructions for adjusting settings, including changing the PIN and managing API keys. The FAQ section addresses common user inquiries related to account security, lesson creation, AI companion functionality, and shadowing exercises. Overall, the document serves as a user manual for navigating and utilizing the ShadowLearn platform effectively.

structure.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
structure.summary: The partial document is a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) section from a user manual for a platform called ShadowLearn. It covers various topics related to account and security, lessons and library, AI companion and memory, and shadowing exercises. Key points include instructions on changing a PIN, data storage details, lesson creation time for YouTube content, troubleshooting errors in lessons, the AI companion's ability to access user data and remember information between sessions, and explanations for skipped segments in shadowing mode. The document aims to assist users in navigating the platform effectively.

pages.content:
ShadowLearn User  Manual
Welcome to ShadowLearn!
Overview
ShadowLearn  is your ultimate companion to conquer any foreign language using the
Shadowing  technique (Listen & Repeat) combined with smart interactive exercises and
AI Assistance.
This guide will walk you through setting up and maximizing the application’ s features.
1. Initial Setup (For  Beginners)
When you launch the app for the first time , you will see a Setup  screen asking for API
Keys  and a PIN. This only happens once.
What are API Keys?
They are “keys” that allow the app to connect with Artificial Intelligence (AI) models to
power Translation, Speech Recognition, and Pronunciation features.
Key Name Main Role Importance
OpenRouter  API KeyTranslates, explains
grammar , and chats with
you.Requir ed
Google Gemini API KeyPowers live voice
conversations in Speak
with AI.Requir ed for  Speak with
AI
  Absolute Security : All keys are encrypted and only saved on your  computer .
The app never sends them to our servers.22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 1/13

How to Get API Keys
OpenRouter  API Key (T ranslation & Explanation)
See tutorial here: https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/Create-API-Key-in-OpenRouter -
95b3cf6d85df49b48ad728d93c8d5b76
Google Gemini API Key (Speak with AI)
Video walkthr ough:  https://app.tango.us/app/workflow/Create-Google-AI-
Studio-API-Key-ac1 10f276682495784c9fa2998323bee
1. Go to aistudio.google.com  and sign in with your Google account
2. Click Get API key  in the left sidebar
3. Click Create API key  → select Create API key in new pr oject  → copy the key
(starts with AIza…)
4. Still inside AI Studio, open Billing  from the left menu → click Add payment
method  → enter your Visa / Master card  details and confirm.   Do not use the fr ee
trial — it is unstable and will cause the voice session to drop or fail. Paid billing is
required for reliable Gemini Live performance.
5. Open ShadowLearn → Settings  → paste the key into the Google Gemini (Realtime)
field and save
Google AI Studio — API key and billing setup
Google AI Studio — API key and billing setup
Cost:  ~300 VNĐ (~$0.012 USD) per minute of conversation. You only pay when
you use it.
Create a PIN
Create a PIN (minimum 4 characters) and confirm it.
The PIN encrypts your keys and lesson data while the app is closed.
Important : If you for get your PIN, you will need to reset the app and re-enter all
API keys. There is no recovery option. Write your PIN somewhere safe!
Setup Screen
Setup Screen
2. Daily Use: Unlocking the App
Every time you re-open ShadowLearn, you will see the Unlock  screen. Simply enter your
PIN to access your lessons and data.
This is normal — it means your data is protected when you’re not using the app.22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 2/13

3. Library & Lesson Management
The main dashboard is the Library , containing all your created lessons.
Library
Library
Creating a New Lesson
Click the + card or Create Lesson  button. You have 2 options:
1. Via YouTube Link  :
Paste the video URL  into the box.
The app will download the audio and generate subtitles automatically .
2. Upload fr om Computer  :
Upload an audio (.mp3) or video (.mp4) file from your device.
Select your tar get language (e.g., English ), then click Generate . The lesson enters a
processing queue — you can return to the Library to wait (status shown as Processing ).
Create New Lesson
Create New Lesson
Managing Existing Lessons  
Each lesson card in the Library supports:
Rename    — Click the pencil icon on a card to give it a new name.
Delete    — Remove the lesson and all its data permanently .
Retry  — If a YouTube lesson failed to process, use Retry to try again.
Sear ch — Use the search bar at the top to filter lessons by title.
4. Learning Interface (Classr oom)  
Opening a lesson divides the screen into 3 main columns :
Shadowing Screen
Shadowing Screen
Left Column — Video / Audio Player
Plays the lesson content. Controls include:
Play / Pause  button
Playback speed : Choose 0.5×, 0.75×, 1×, 1.25×, or 1.5× — slower speeds are great
for beginners
Seek bar : Click anywhere on the timeline to jump to that moment
Volume slider : Adjust audio volume
Download  ⬇: Save the video/audio file to your co mputer
Rename   : Click the pencil icon next to the lesson title to rename it22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 3/13

Middle Column — Subtitles & Practice
Displays all segments with Pinyin , Chinese text , and translation .
Click any segment  to jump the video to that exact moment.
Hover  a word  to see its pronunciation and a bookmark icon.
Bookmark : Save any word to your Vocabulary Workbook.
Play : Hear the AI pronounce any segment.
Copy : Copy the segment text to clipboard.
Shadow : Start a Shadowing session beginning from that specific segment.
Shadowing button  at the top: Start a full Shadowing session for the lesson.
Right Column — AI Companion (2 Tabs)
The right column is home to your personal AI tutor — an intelligent agent that knows
your vocabulary , tracks your progress, and runs interactive exercises directly inside the
chat. See AI Companion ↓  for the full guide.
Tab 1 — Chat — Talk to your AI tutor , ask questions, request exercises. Tab 2 —
Workbook — Browse words saved from this lesson; click any word to jump to it in the
transcript. Study This Lesson  button launches a quiz session.
5. AI Companion — Your Personal Language Tutor
Most language apps give you exer cises. ShadowLearn gives you a tutor .
The AI Companion  lives in the right panel of every lesson. It is not a simple chatbot —
it is an intelligent, agentic tutor that knows who you ar e, remembers you acr oss
sessions , and takes action  on your behalf. It can read your vocabulary list, check which
words you’re due to review , see exactly which sentence you’re studying, and then launch
a real interactive exercise — all directly inside the chat.
What Makes It Different
Ordinary chatbot ShadowLearn AI Companion
Answers questions Answers questions and acts on them
Forgets you next sessionRemembers  your preferences, struggles,
and goals
Gives generic adviceAdapts to your  level and pr ogress in real
time
Talks about exercises Runs exer cises dir ectly in the chat
How to Start a Conversation
Just type naturally . Here are ideas to get you going:
Ask about the curr ent sentence:  - “What does this sentence mean?”  - “Break down the
grammar of this line for me.”  - “What’ s the differ ence between 了  (le) and 过  (guò)?”22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 4/13

Ask for  practice:  - “Quiz me on the wor ds I saved fr om this lesson.”  - “Give me a
dictation exer cise.”  - “Can we practice the pr onunciation of this sentence?”  - “I keep
forgetting 尤其  — can we drill it?”
Ask for  a pr ogress check:  - “How am I doing overall?”  - “Show me my accuracy
trend.”  - “What wor ds should I r eview today?”
Tell it about yourself:  - “I’m a complete beginner , please keep explanations simple.”  -
“My goal is to pass HSK 3 by the end of the year .” - “I struggle with tones — focus on
that when you quiz me.”
The AI will remember everything you tell it, even in future sessions.
Interactive Exercises Right in the Chat
This is the standout feature. When you ask for practice, the AI doesn’ t just describe an
exercise — it renders a live, interactive exer cise dir ectly inside the conversation . You
complete it, submit your answer , and the AI responds with feedback and updates your
progress automatically .
Exercises the AI can launch in chat:
Exer cise What happens
DictationAn audio clip plays; you type what you
hear
  Character  WritingA character appears; you trace its stroke
order
Pinyin Recall A character appears; you type its pinyin
Translation A sentence appears; you translate it
PronunciationA sentence appears; you record yourself
saying it
Cloze (Fill-in-the-blank)The AI writes a short story using your
words with gaps to fill
ReconstructionWord chips appear scrambled; you drag
them into the right order
The AI chooses which words to include based on what you’ve saved, what’ s due for
review , and what you’ve been struggling with recently — not randomly .
It Knows You — The Learner Profile
The first time you start chatting, the AI will ask you a few quick questions: - What is your
current level? - What is your native language? - What is your daily study goal?
These answers build your Learner  Profile, which the AI reads at the start of every
session to calibrate the dif ficulty of its explanations and exercise selections. You can
update it at any time simply by telling the AI — “I think I’m at intermediate level now”
— and it will adjust.22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 5/13

Long-Term Memory  
The AI keeps a persistent memory  of important things you share with it: - Your learning
goals and motivations - Topics or grammar patterns you find dif ficult - Preferences for
how you like to be taught - Observations it makes about your performance over time
This memory carries over to every future session. The AI can also search its own memory
when you ask — “What have I told you about my goals?”  — and retrieve what it knows
about you.
Ask for Your Progress
At any time, type: - “Show me my pr ogress chart.”  - “How is my skill br eakdown
looking?”
The AI will render a visual chart  right in the chat — an accuracy trend graph or a
mastery breakdown across all five skills (Listening, Speaking, Reading, Writing,
Vocabulary).
Tips for Getting the Best Out of It
Be specific.  “Quiz me on 想  and 觉得 ” works better than “quiz me” .
Tell it your  mood.  “I only have 5 minutes”  → it will keep the session short.
Ask it to explain differ ently . If an explanation doesn’ t click, say “explain that
another way” .
Trust its suggestions.  When it says “you have 3 wor ds due for r eview” , take the quiz
— spaced repetition works best when you don’ t skip.
6. Speak with AI — Live Voice Practice  
No scheduling. No judgment. Just you and an AI that corr ects your grammar while
you speak.
Speak with AI is a real-time voice conversation feature. You speak — the AI responds in
your tar get language — and the app gives you instant support so you never get stuck.
Before using this feature, make sure you’ve added your Google Gemini API key  in
Settings (see Initial Setup ↑ ).
What You Get in Every Session
Featur e What it does
Voice conversationSpeak freely; AI responds naturally in
your tar get language
Grammar  feedbackEvery message silently analyzed — tap it
to see corrections
Next-Line SuggestionCan’ t think of what to say? AI suggests
your next line22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 6/13

Featur e What it does
Cultural tipsNotes on idioms, formality , and cultural
nuance
Vocabulary checklist Target words auto-ticked as you use them
Session r ecapFull summary with all grammar
corrections and an overall evaluation
Supported languages:  Chinese (Simplified), English, Japanese. Session limit:  10
minutes per session (countdown timer shown during conversation).
How to Use It
Step 1 — Open Speak with AI
There are two ways to access the feature:
From the Topbar:  Click the Speak with AI button in the left navigation menu.
From the Companion Panel:  Inside a lesson, click the Voice icon in the AI
Companion panel on the right.
If your Google Gemini key isn’ t set yet, the app will prompt you to go to Settings first.
Two entry points — sidebar and Companion Panel
Two entry points — sidebar and Companion Panel
Step 2 — Choose language and level
Select your target language , then pick a proficiency level :
Beginner  — HSK 1-2 / CEFR A1-A2 / JLPT  N5-N4 / TOPIK I
Intermediate  — HSK 3-4 / CEFR B1-B2 / JLPT  N3-N2 / TOPIK II 3-4
Advanced  — HSK 5-6 / CEFR C1-C2 / JLPT  N1 / TOPIK II 5-6
The AI adjusts vocabulary , sentence complexity , and feedback depth to match your level.
Click Continue .
Language and level picker
Language and level picker
Step 3 — Pick a persona
Choose the AI character you want to talk to. Each persona has a distinct personality and
speaking style — friendly study buddy , encouraging friend, strict parent, taxi driver , and
more. Available personas vary by language.
Persona picker — grid of characters

Step 4 — Pick a situation
Choose a scenario from the grid: casual chat, ordering food, asking directions,
shopping/bar gaining, job interview , meeting the family , hospital visit, karaoke, and more.
Can’t find the right scenario?  Click ** Create your own** at the top of the grid.
Describe your situation in plain text and the AI generates a custom scenario with
matching vocabulary .
Situation picker
Situation picker
Step 5 — Pr eview and confirm
Before starting, review the scenario card: description, your role and goal, and the tar get
vocabulary for this session. Click Regenerate  for a fresh version, or Start  when ready .
Situation preview card
Situation preview card
Step 6 — Have the conversation
The session screen has three panels:
Center  — Conversation  - AI avatar with a mic ring that pulses when the AI is speaking -
Live transcript of the full conversation - Hold micr ophone button to speak, r elease
when done  - Session countdown timer from 10:00
Right — Intelligence Panel  - Target vocabulary checklist  — words get crossed of f as
you use them - Next-Line Suggestion  — a contextual prompt to help continue the
conversation - Cultural tips  — idioms, register , situational etiquette - Vocab tips  —
quick explanations of words the AI just used
Left — Grammar  Feedback  - Click any message in the transcript to open its grammar
panel - Corrections shown as: original  → corrected , with explanation
Active session — all three panels labeled
Active session — all three panels labeled
Tips: Speak at a natural pace. If you go silent, the AI gently prompts you. Use
Next-Line Suggestion when stuck — say it aloud and see how the AI responds.
Grammar corrections appear automatically .
When finished, click End Session .
Step 7 — Review your  recap
After the session, a full recap shows: - Duration and turn count - Overall AI evaluation of
your performance - Strengths and areas to improve - Grammar correction timeline —
every correction from the session, in order
Click Practice Again  to repeat the same scenario, or Back  to choose a new one.

Troubleshooting
“Google API key r equir ed” err or → Go to Settings and add your Google Gemini API
key.
Micr ophone not working  → In Chrome/Edge: click the lock icon in the address bar →
allow microphone.
Session won’t start / loading spinner  hangs  → Check your internet connection. The
session requires a stable connection to the voice server .
Choppy or  delayed audio  → Close other tabs using audio. Move closer to your router or
switch to a wired connection.
7. Shadowing Practice (Pr onunciation & Listening)
Click the Shadowing  button in the middle column (or the icon on a specific segment). A
dialog will appear where you choose your mode and how many segments to practice.
Choosing a Mode
Shadowing Modes
Shadowing Modes
Mode What You Do
Dictation ModeListen to a segment and type what you
hear
Speaking ModeListen to a segment and speak it aloud
into your microphone
You can also set how many segments  to practice, or choose “All remaining” to go
through the whole lesson.
Dictation Mode Flow
Step 1 — Listen Phase  - The segment audio plays automatically . - Focus on the sounds,
rhythm, and tone. Use the Replay  button or press Space  to hear it again.
Step 2 — Type Phase  - A text input appears. Type exactly what you heard. - Click
Submit  when done.
Step 3 — Reveal Phase  - Your answer is shown alongside the correct text. - Each
character is highlighted: Green = correct, Red = wrong. - Your accuracy score is shown. -
Click Retry  to try again or Next  to move on.
Dictation Result
Dictation Result22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
ﬁle:///home/ross-geller/Projects/personal/shadowing-companion/frontend/public/docs/USER_MANUAL.html 9/13

Speaking Mode Flow
Step 1 — Listen Phase  - The segment audio plays automatically . Press Space  or Replay
to hear it again.
Step 2 — Speak Phase  - Click the micr ophone button  (or press Space ) to start
recording. - Speak the segment aloud clearly , then click again to stop. - You can play back
your recording before submitting, or click Redo  to re-record. - Click Submit  when
satisfied.
Step 3 — Reveal Phase  - Your recording is played back alongside the correct AI
pronunciation. - Azure grades your pronunciation: Green = good, Red = needs work. -
Click Retry  to try again or Next  to continue.
Shadowing Session Summary
After all segments are done, you see a full summary: - Accuracy score per segment -
Which segments you attempted, skipped, or were auto-skipped (very short segments are
skipped automatically) - Overall session average score
8. Vocabulary Workbook, Quizzes & Pr ogress
Saving Words
While reading subtitles, hover  over  any word  and click the Bookmark icon. The word is
instantly added to your Workbook.
Accessing the Full Workbook
Click Workbook  in the top navigation to open the full Vocabulary page. Here you can:
See total words saved  and how many lessons they come from
Sear ch for any word by character , pinyin, or meaning
Browse words grouped by lesson  — click a group to expand or collapse it
Click Play on any word to hear it pronounced
Click Study  on any lesson group to practice only those words

How scoring works:  Binary exercises (Dictation, Cloze, Reconstruction, Pinyin
Recall) score 100 for correct or 0 for wrong. Character Writing scores 100 if no
hints were used, 80 if hints were used. Translation and Pronunciation receive real
numeric scores (0–100) from AI. A score of 60 or  above  counts as a correct
answer for accuracy purposes.
Common Mistakes
Highlights vocabulary items you have struggled with most frequently , so you know
where to focus.
Spaced Repetition & Review Queue
Every time you answer an exercise, ShadowLearn schedules the next review for that
vocabulary word using a scientifically-proven method that spaces reviews over increasing
intervals based on how well you know the word:
Answer correctly and confidently → next review in several days
Answer incorrectly → reviewed again soon (tomorrow or the day after)
Consistently correct over 5 sessions → the word levels up and is reviewed less often
When words are due for review , a banner appears at the top of the Workbook page: “X
items due for  review today” . Click Start Review  to launch a tar geted session with only
those words.

by default
Lock App — immediately lock the app (requires PIN to re-open)

10. Fr equently Asked Questions (F AQ)
Account & Security
Q: Can I change my PIN?  - A: Yes — open Settings and use the Change PIN  section.
Write your PIN down somewhere safe; there is no recovery if for gotten.
Q: Wher e is my data stor ed? - A: Everything — lessons, vocabulary , chat history , AI
memory , audio cache — is stored locally in your browser ’s storage. Nothing is sent to our
servers.
Lessons & Library
Q: How long does YouTube lesson cr eation take?  - A: Usually 1–3 minutes depending
on video length (download + transcription + translation).
Q: The lesson shows an err or. What do I do?  - A: For YouTube lessons, click Retry  on
the lesson card in the Library . For uploaded files, delete the lesson and upload again.
AI Companion & Memory
Q: The AI Companion doesn’t seem to know about my lesson / vocabulary . - A: The
AI looks up your data when it needs it (when you ask for a quiz or progress check). Just
ask directly — “quiz me on my saved wor ds” — and it will fetch them.
Q: Can the AI Companion r emember  things between sessions?  - A: Yes. It has a long-
term memory system. Tell it your goals, level, or problem areas and it will remember for
future sessions.
Shadowing & Exercises
Q: Why ar e some segments skipped in Shadowing mode?  - A: Very short segments
(fewer than 3 words) are automatically skipped — they are too brief to practice
meaningfully .
Wishing you pr oductive and enjoyable learning sessions on ShadowLearn!22/05/2026, 09:57 ShadowLearn User Manual
